title: Refactor with guard composition using in [...] and when
prompt:
Given:

```
def role_color(user) do
  cond do
    user.role == "owner" or user.role == "admin" -> "red"
    user.role == "editor" or user.role == "contributor" -> "blue"
    user.role == "viewer" or user.role == "guest" -> "gray"
    true -> "black"
  end
end
```

Rewrite as function heads with `when role in [...]` guard composition. Each head pattern-matches `%{role: role}` and uses `when role in ["owner", "admin"]`, etc. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.RoleColor do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Using `in [...]` in a guard is less common than individual equality checks. Multi-step: match + destructure + guard.
